description: ATX-1 regulatory cross-reference matrix mapping techniques to NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, OWASP LLM Top 10, and ATM-1 threat scenarios.

techniques.name: Non-Owner Instruction Compliance

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Legal and regulatory requirements), MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms to manage AI risks)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management system — authority verification as risk control; Art. 14 Human oversight — owner authorization for destructive actions

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent executes destructive operations based on user instructions that conflict with owner policy

techniques.name: Implicit Authority Acceptance

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI characteristics), MAP 3.4 (Risks from third-party components and data)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — instruction provenance as risk control; Art. 15 Accuracy and robustness — resistance to instruction injection

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent treats forwarded messages as direct instructions from the apparent sender

techniques.name: Mass Distribution Under Spoofed Authority

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Legal and regulatory requirements), MANAGE 2.4 (Risk treatments proportionate to impact)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — distribution controls; Art. 14 Human oversight — escalation for mass actions

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent distributes content to recipients based on a spoofed authority claim in a prompt

techniques.name: Irreversible Collateral Action

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Risk management mechanisms), MEASURE 2.6 (Impact assessment of AI actions)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — proportionality assessment; Art. 14 Human oversight — confirmation for irreversible actions

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent permanently deletes data when the objective only requires filtering or archival

techniques.name: Social Pressure Escalation

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI — safe, fair), MEASURE 2.11 (Fairness and non-discrimination)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 5 Prohibited AI practices — manipulation and exploitation; Art. 9 Risk management — user dignity safeguards

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent induces emotional distress in users through escalating social pressure tactics

techniques.name: Guilt-Induced Self-Restriction

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices for AI risk management), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — configuration integrity; Art. 15 Accuracy — preventing capability degradation

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent restricts its own future capabilities based on guilt rather than policy

techniques.name: Persistent Process Injection

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms for managing AI risks), MEASURE 2.5 (Computational resource monitoring)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — resource boundary enforcement; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing resource exhaustion

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent spawns persistent background processes that survive session boundaries

techniques.name: Inter-Agent Conversational Loop

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms for managing AI risks), MEASURE 2.5 (Computational resource monitoring)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — loop detection as risk control; Art. 15 Robustness — termination guarantees

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Two or more agents enter conversational loops that consume unbounded compute

techniques.name: Storage Exhaustion via Memory Accumulation

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms for managing AI risks), MEASURE 2.5 (Storage resource monitoring)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — storage quota enforcement; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing storage exhaustion

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent accumulates context, logs, or artifacts without storage limits or lifecycle management

techniques.name: Bulk Data Disclosure via Indirect Request

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Legal and regulatory requirements — data protection), MANAGE 2.2 (Data disclosure controls)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — data access controls; Art. 10 Data governance — authorized recipient scope; Art. 14 Human oversight — disclosure authorization

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent exports complete datasets in response to indirect requests that do not carry owner authorization

techniques.name: Semantic Sensitivity Bypass

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Data protection regulations), MANAGE 2.2 (Immutable classification enforcement)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — classification integrity; Art. 10 Data governance — structural data sensitivity

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent discloses sensitive information by reclassifying it as non-sensitive based on semantic reasoning

techniques.name: Urgency-Induced Disclosure

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI characteristics — secure), MANAGE 2.2 (Override governance mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — urgency verification; Art. 14 Human oversight — override controls requiring human escalation

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent overrides disclosure controls when presented with fabricated urgency

techniques.name: Session Boundary Identity Reset

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI — secure, accountable), MAP 3.5 (Risks from session management)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — identity persistence; Art. 14 Human oversight — identity verification; Art. 15 Robustness — session boundary security

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent loses track of principal identity across session boundaries, treating each session as a fresh trust context

techniques.name: Display Name Authority Claim

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI — secure), MAP 3.4 (Risks from spoofable identity signals)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — cryptographic identity verification; Art. 14 Human oversight — verified authority chains

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent grants authority to an instruction based on a display name or email header rather than a verified identity credential

techniques.name: Externally Editable Governance Injection

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices — configuration management), MANAGE 2.2 (Governance integrity mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — governance tamper-evidence; Art. 15 Accuracy and robustness — immutable governance configuration

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent governance rules stored in files accessible to external tools or other agents are silently modified

techniques.name: Covert Constitutional Amendment

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices — change management), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring for governance drift)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — self-modification detection; Art. 12 Record-keeping — audit trail for governance changes; Art. 14 Human oversight — amendment authorization

techniques.atm_1_scenario: An agent modifies its own constitutional constraints through indirect means that bypass audit logging

techniques.name: Tribal Knowledge Transfer

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from third-party practices), MANAGE 2.2 (Practice provenance verification)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — independent governance derivation; Art. 15 Robustness — resistance to unverified behavioral adoption

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent adopts operational practices from another agent without verifying their provenance or authorization

techniques.name: Compromised Governance Propagation

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from shared infrastructure), MANAGE 2.2 (Governance isolation mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — governance isolation; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing cascading governance compromise

techniques.atm_1_scenario: A governance compromise in one agent propagates to others through shared configuration or imitation

techniques.name: False Deletion Confirmation

nist_ai_rmf.description: MEASURE 2.6 (Outcome verification), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring — truthful reporting)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 13 Transparency — accurate system state reporting; Art. 14 Human oversight — outcome verification before confirmation

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent confirms deletion of records that were not actually deleted; reports task completion based on intent rather than verified outcome

techniques.name: Silent Provider-Level Task Failure

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from provider constraints), MANAGE 4.2 (Mechanisms for constraint layer visibility)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 13 Transparency — constraint layer visibility; Art. 14 Human oversight — failure signal propagation to operators

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Provider-imposed content filters silently block agent actions without notification; rate limits cause partial task completion with no indication of incompleteness
